ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.info: This attribute defines the context of the transaction.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.action.info:
The sender initiates the Beckn protocol method, which is then carried out by the receiver.
 Beckn is an open, interoperable and universal transaction protocol to enable a decentralised digital economy.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.bap_id.info: This the subscriber ID of the BAP. It is the unique id for the buyer app.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.bap_uri.info: This is the subscriber URL of the Buyer App (BAP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from Seller App (BPPs).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.bpp_id.info: It explains the Explains the subscriber of the Seller App (BPP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.bpp_uri.info: The subscriber URL of the Seller App (BPP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from the Buyer App (BAP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.domain.info: This is the domain code relevant to the context of this transaction. Here, it is "ONDC:FIS12".

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.locatio.info: This attribute defines the location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute defines the city of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute describes the city code relevant to the location or the area in which it is situated.
This attribute defines the country of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.

It Explains the country code in accordance with the ISO 3166-1 and ISO 3166-2 formats.
 
 The ISO 3166-1 standard includes 249 countries, with 193 sovereign states as UN members. Dependent territories in ISO 3166-1 are often detailed as subdivisions in ISO 3166-2 under their administering state.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.message.info:
This is a true and unique identifier that persists consistently throughout a request/callback cycle.
 This value can be utilized to filter out and ignore duplicate messages coming from the BPP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.timesta.info:
It specifies the time of request generation in RFC3339 format. RFC3339 is a date and time format defined by the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) in the RFC 3339 standard.
 YYYY: Four-digit year
 MM: Two-digit month (01-12)
 DD: Two-digit day of the month (01-31)
 T: Separator between the date and time
 HH: Two-digit hour (00-23)
 MM: Two-digit minute (00-59)
 SS: Two-digit second (00-59)
 .sss: Fractional seconds (optional)
 Z: Indicates the time is in UTC (optional; the time may also include an offset)

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.transac.info:
It describes a unique identifier that remains consistent across all API calls from search to confirm. 
 It shows an active user session across multiple requests. BPPs can use it to send personalized recommendations and dynamic offers, even if they don't know the user active on the BAP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.ttl.info:
Specifies the validity duration of this message in ISO8601 format, after timestamp.
 In the case of "PT30S," it represents a duration of 30 seconds. Here's a breakdown of the components:
 
 - "P" indicates the period.
 - "T" indicates the time part.
 - "30S" represents 30 seconds.
 
 So, "PT30S" signifies a duration of 30 seconds.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.context.version.info: It explains the transaction protocol version utilized by the sender.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.message.order.info: This refers to the deatils about the order.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.message.order.f.info: This describes the details about the fulfillments
This describes the unique id of the fulfillment

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.confirm.message.order.i.info: Describe the details of the item that the consumer wants to order
This describes the unique id of the item
This defines the unique id of the parent item

Describes the payments tag group as ACCOUNT_DETAILS

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.info: This attribute defines the context of the transaction.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.actio.info:
The sender initiates the Beckn protocol method, which is then carried out by the receiver.
 Beckn is an open, interoperable and universal transaction protocol to enable a decentralised digital economy.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.bap_i.info: This the subscriber ID of the BAP. It is the unique id for the buyer app.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.bap_u.info: This is the subscriber URL of the Buyer App (BAP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from Seller App (BPPs).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.bpp_i.info: It explains the Explains the subscriber of the Seller App (BPP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.bpp_u.info: The subscriber URL of the Seller App (BPP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from the Buyer App (BAP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.domai.info: This is the domain code relevant to the context of this transaction. Here, it is "ONDC:FIS12".

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.locat.info: This attribute defines the location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute defines the city of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute describes the city code relevant to the location or the area in which it is situated.
This attribute defines the country of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.

It Explains the country code in accordance with the ISO 3166-1 and ISO 3166-2 formats.
 
 The ISO 3166-1 standard includes 249 countries, with 193 sovereign states as UN members. Dependent territories in ISO 3166-1 are often detailed as subdivisions in ISO 3166-2 under their administering state.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.messa.info:
This is a true and unique identifier that persists consistently throughout a request/callback cycle.
 This value can be utilized to filter out and ignore duplicate messages coming from the BPP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.times.info:
It specifies the time of request generation in RFC3339 format. RFC3339 is a date and time format defined by the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) in the RFC 3339 standard.
 YYYY: Four-digit year
 MM: Two-digit month (01-12)
 DD: Two-digit day of the month (01-31)
 T: Separator between the date and time
 HH: Two-digit hour (00-23)
 MM: Two-digit minute (00-59)
 SS: Two-digit second (00-59)
 .sss: Fractional seconds (optional)
 Z: Indicates the time is in UTC (optional; the time may also include an offset)

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.trans.info:
It describes a unique identifier that remains consistent across all API calls from search to confirm. 
 It shows an active user session across multiple requests. BPPs can use it to send personalized recommendations and dynamic offers, even if they don't know the user active on the BAP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.ttl.info:
Specifies the validity duration of this message in ISO8601 format, after timestamp.
 In the case of "PT30S," it represents a duration of 30 seconds. Here's a breakdown of the components:
 
 - "P" indicates the period.
 - "T" indicates the time part.
 - "30S" represents 30 seconds.
 
 So, "PT30S" signifies a duration of 30 seconds.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.context.versi.info: It explains the transaction protocol version utilized by the sender.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.on_search.message.info: This attribute refers to the message of the transaction

 Beckn is an open, interoperable and universal transaction protocol to enable a decentralised digital economy.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.bap_id.info: This the subscriber ID of the BAP. It is the unique id for the buyer app.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.bap_uri.info: This is the subscriber URL of the Buyer App (BAP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from Seller App (BPPs).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.bpp_id.info: It explains the Explains the subscriber of the Seller App (BPP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.bpp_uri.info: The subscriber URL of the Seller App (BPP) is the endpoint that accepts callbacks from the Buyer App (BAP).

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.domain.info: This is the domain code relevant to the context of this transaction. Here, it is "ONDC:FIS12".

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.location.info: This attribute defines the location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute defines the city of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.
This attribute describes the city code relevant to the location or the area in which it is situated.
This attribute defines the country of location where the transaction is meant to be completed.

It Explains the country code in accordance with the ISO 3166-1 and ISO 3166-2 formats.
 
 The ISO 3166-1 standard includes 249 countries, with 193 sovereign states as UN members. Dependent territories in ISO 3166-1 are often detailed as subdivisions in ISO 3166-2 under their administering state.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.message_.info:
This is a true and unique identifier that persists consistently throughout a request/callback cycle.
 This value can be utilized to filter out and ignore duplicate messages coming from the BPP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.timestam.info:
It specifies the time of request generation in RFC3339 format. RFC3339 is a date and time format defined by the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) in the RFC 3339 standard.
 YYYY: Four-digit year
 MM: Two-digit month (01-12)
 DD: Two-digit day of the month (01-31)
 T: Separator between the date and time
 HH: Two-digit hour (00-23)
 MM: Two-digit minute (00-59)
 SS: Two-digit second (00-59)
 .sss: Fractional seconds (optional)
 Z: Indicates the time is in UTC (optional; the time may also include an offset)

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.transact.info:
It describes a unique identifier that remains consistent across all API calls from search to confirm. 
 It shows an active user session across multiple requests. BPPs can use it to send personalized recommendations and dynamic offers, even if they don't know the user active on the BAP.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.ttl.info:
Specifies the validity duration of this message in ISO8601 format, after timestamp.
 In the case of "PT30S," it represents a duration of 30 seconds. Here's a breakdown of the components:
 
 - "P" indicates the period.
 - "T" indicates the time part.
 - "30S" represents 30 seconds.
 
 So, "PT30S" signifies a duration of 30 seconds.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.context.version.info: It explains the transaction protocol version utilized by the sender.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.message.info: This attribute refers to the message of the transaction

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.message.intent.info: This attribute refers to intent to buy or avail a product or a service. The BAP can declare the intent of the consumer containing

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.message.intent.c.info: This attribute refers to details on the item category.
This attribute refers to the category descriptor code
This attribute refers to categorizing loans based on whether they are intended for personal use or are meant for invoices.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.message.intent.p.info: This field refers to the details of the payment.
This field indicates the payment collector. If set to 'bap,' BAP collects first and settles with BPP. If BPP agrees, no payment link is sent. If set to 'bpp,' BPP collects payment directly.
Describe the provider from which the customer wants to place to the order from
This refers to the unique id of the loan provider
Describe the details of the item that the consumer wants to order
This describes the unique id of the item
Describes the additional input required from the seller to purchase / avail this item
Describes the business pan name details
Describes the nature of business details

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.search.message.intent.t.info: This attribute refers to the tags
This attribute refers to the tags descriptor
This attribute refers to the descriptor code
This attribute refers to the name of the tags
This attribute refers to the dispaly property of the tags
This attribute refers to the list of tags of a tag group
This attribute refers to the list of tags of a tag group
This attribute refers to the lists descriptor code
This attribute refers to the name of the tags

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.select.context.info: This attribute defines the context of the transaction.

ONDC:FIS12.2.3.0.select.context.action.info:
The sender initiates the Beckn protocol method, which is then carried out by the receiver.
 Beckn is an open, interoperable and universal transaction protocol to enable a decentralised digital economy.
